arts.ttl: McGinn, Cash, Mings - Aston Villa injury latest and return dates ahead of Ipswich Town clash - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Aston Villa will be without four players for their trip to Ipswich Town this weekend, including long-term absentees Tyrone Mings and Boubacar Kamara. John McGinn and Matty Cash are the other absentees, while Jaden Philogene and Diego Carlos could both come back into the squad to face Ipswich after they trained at Bodymoor Heath in the lead up to the game.

arts.ttl: I hope I'm wrong but Aston Villa face two key stumbling blocks to Champions League dream
arts.sum: “When you're one of the clubs that end up in the Champions League on a regular basis, you build a squad that has the mentality for coping with the extra psychological pressure, as well as the football side of it. Despite injuries suffered to his team in the last campaign, Villa managed to qualify for Champions League football, but Allardyce claims the "stress" of playing in Europe's elite club competition could take its toll.

Fixtures Ipswich Town Vs. Aston Villa - Sun, Sep 29th 2024, 14:00 Aston Villa travel to Ipswich Town on Sunday aiming to continue their impressive start to the season. The Villans have won six of their opening seven matches so far this term, including all four on the road since an opening-day triumph at West Ham United. Unai Emery and his side have been going through their preparations at Bodymoor Heath, as Villa get set for their first trip to Portman Road for six years and first top-flight encounter against the Tractor Boys since 2002. You can view images from training below...

arts.sum: Both Unai Emery and Eddie Howe can be rightly pleased by the way in which Aston Villa and Newcastle respectively have begun their 2024/25 campaigns. arts.ttl: John McGinn Aston Villa injury update as Unai Emery hatches plan for return
arts.sum: "We have our challenges and it is to be competitive in the Premier League and keep the same consistency that we had last year," Emery said. Villa head to Ipswich on Sunday in search of their fifth win in six Premier League games, while they will host Crystal Palace next month for a place in the Carabao Cup quarter-finals after also making the perfect start to the Champions League with a 3-0 win at BSC Young Boys earlier in September.

Fixtures Manchester City U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Sep 27th 2024, 19:00 Aston Villa fell to a 4-1 defeat against Manchester City in Premier League 2 on Friday night. The hosts raced into a three-goal lead before the break, with Emilio Lawrence, Farid Alfa-Ruprecht and Will Dickson on target during the first half at the Joie Stadium. Kobei Moore reduced the arrears in the 72nd minute but substitute Justin Oboavwoduo added a fourth for City in the latter stages. The Young Lions will be looking to get back on track when they face Bayern Munich in the UEFA Youth League on Wednesday afternoon. Villa started well and Kadan Young, having made his first-team debut in Tuesday’s Carabao victory at Wycombe Wanderers, fired a right-footed effort against a post after just five minutes. Todd Alcock and Triston Rowe both threatened in the final third as Moore also headed just over the crossbar for the impressive visitors. But it was City who opened the scoring in the 18th minute as Lawrence applied a well-taken finish into the bottom corner. And the home side doubled their lead shortly afterwards as Alfa-Ruprecht converted from close range, before Dickson added a third in the 36th minute to leave Villa facing a three-goal deficit at the interval. Kane Taylor fired wide just before the hour mark as Villa, enjoying a spell of dominance, looked for a route back into the encounter. And they got it with 18 minutes remaining as Moore fired home a rebound from City goalkeeper Jack Wint’s parried save. Taylor then saw his strike blocked before City ended any hopes of a fightback as Oboavwoduo converted from the edge of the penalty area in the 85th minute. Moore again went close late on for Villa but they were left to taste defeat in the PL2 encounter, with Josep Gombau’s side having collected seven points from their opening five league matches of the campaign. arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: McKenna on Aston Villa, Unai Emery and Ollie Watkins
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: Kieran McKenna says Aston Villa counterpart Unai Emery is a manager he admires a lot. Villa's attack is spearheaded by England international Ollie Watkins, a player who has been directly involved in 57 Premier League goals (20 assists, 37 scored) since the start of the 2022/23 season.

arts.ttl: Joleon Lescott reveals the part Jack Grealish played in his infamous 2016 car tweet - when the defender was slammed for 'accidentally' posting a picture of a £121,000 Mercedes after Aston Villa's 6-0 defeat by Liverpool
arts.sum: Lescott infuriated already-irate supporters after tweeting a photo of a £121,000 Mercedes following a 6-0 thrashing against Liverpool with no caption or context.
